title: Troubleshooting Basics
drDigitalIntro: Troubleshooting means figuring out why something isn't working and fixing it. The golden rule: try the simplest fix first. Restart the app. If that doesn't work, restart the Mac. If still broken, check if you're connected to the internet. Search the error message online — you're never the first person to have this problem. Stay calm; almost every problem has a solution.

playgroundTask.question: An app on your Mac has frozen and won't respond. What should you try FIRST?

options.text: Take the Mac to a repair shop immediately
Force quit the frozen app and reopen it
Delete the app and buy a new Mac

drDigitalSuccess: Force quit (Cmd+Option+Esc) closes a frozen app without restarting. Start with the simplest fix before escalating!
drDigitalHint: What's the least drastic thing you can try that might fix a frozen app?
